Role.title: Role
Role.description: Defines user roles within the application, controlling access to different functionalities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

name.description: The name of the role (e.g., 'Admin', 'Warehouse Manager', 'Operator').

description.description: A detailed description of the role's permissions and responsibilities.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the role was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the role was last updated.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents application users, linking to roles and specific warehouses. Authentication details are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. This ID is distinct from any external authentication system ID.

username.description: The user's chosen username or display name.

roleId.description: Reference to the Role assigned to this user. (Relationship: Role 1:N User)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse this user is primarily associated with, if applicable. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N User)

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user was soft-deleted, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user was last updated.

Warehouse.title: Warehouse
Warehouse.description: Represents a physical or logical location for storing products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Warehouse entity.

address.description: The physical address of the warehouse.

responsiblePersonId.description: Reference to the User who is responsible for this warehouse. (Relationship: User 1:N Warehouse)

phone.description: Contact phone number for the warehouse.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the warehouse was soft-deleted, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the warehouse was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the warehouse was last updated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category.

description.description: A description of the product category.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was soft-deleted, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Supplier.title: Supplier
Supplier.description: Represents a vendor or supplier from whom products are sourced.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Supplier entity.

contactPerson.description: The name of the primary contact person at the supplier.

phone.description: Contact phone number for the supplier.

email.description: Contact email address for the supplier.

address.description: The physical address of the supplier.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the supplier was soft-deleted, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the supplier was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Defines individual products managed in the inventory system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category the product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

unitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the product is measured (e.g., 'pcs', 'kg', 'liter').

costPrice.description: The cost price of the product (tannarx).

salePrice.description: The selling price of the product (sotuv narxi).

lowStockThreshold.description: The minimum quantity threshold for this product, triggering low-stock alerts.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was soft-deleted, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product was last updated.

GoodsReceipt.title: GoodsReceipt
GoodsReceipt.description: Records the process of receiving goods into a warehouse (Stock In).

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsReceipt entity.

deliveryNoteNumber.description: Unique delivery note number (nakladnoy) for this receipt.

date.description: The date when the goods were received.

supplierId.description: Reference to the Supplier from whom the goods were received. (Relationship: Supplier 1:N GoodsReceipt)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse where the goods were received. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N GoodsReceipt)

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User responsible for recording this goods receipt. (Relationship: User 1:N GoodsReceipt)

description.description: Optional notes or description for the goods receipt.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt record was last updated.

GoodsReceiptItem.title: GoodsReceiptItem
GoodsReceiptItem.description: Details individual products and quantities received within a GoodsReceipt.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsReceiptItem entity.

goodsReceiptId.description: Reference to the parent GoodsReceipt. (Relationship: GoodsReceipt 1:N GoodsReceiptItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being received. (Relationship: Product 1:N GoodsReceiptItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product received.

unitPrice.description: The unit price of the product at the time of receipt.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods receipt item record was last updated.

GoodsIssue.title: GoodsIssue
GoodsIssue.description: Records the process of issuing goods from a warehouse (Stock Out).

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsIssue entity.

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse from which the goods were issued. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N GoodsIssue)

destination.description: The destination of the issued goods (e.g., client name, department).

date.description: The date when the goods were issued.

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User responsible for recording this goods issue. (Relationship: User 1:N GoodsIssue)

description.description: Optional notes or description for the goods issue.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue record was last updated.

GoodsIssueItem.title: GoodsIssueItem
GoodsIssueItem.description: Details individual products and quantities issued within a GoodsIssue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoodsIssueItem entity.

goodsIssueId.description: Reference to the parent GoodsIssue. (Relationship: GoodsIssue 1:N GoodsIssueItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being issued. (Relationship: Product 1:N GoodsIssueItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product issued.

unitPrice.description: The unit price of the product at the time of issue.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the goods issue item record was last updated.

Transfer.title: Transfer
Transfer.description: Records the movement of goods between two warehouses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transfer entity.

sourceWarehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse from which products are transferred. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N Transfer - as source)

destinationWarehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse to which products are transferred. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N Transfer - as destination)

date.description: The date when the transfer was initiated or completed.

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User responsible for initiating this transfer. (Relationship: User 1:N Transfer)

description.description: Optional notes or description for the transfer.

status.description: The current status of the transfer (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'cancelled').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer record was last updated.

TransferItem.title: TransferItem
TransferItem.description: Details individual products and quantities involved in a Transfer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransferItem entity.

transferId.description: Reference to the parent Transfer. (Relationship: Transfer 1:N TransferItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being transferred. (Relationship: Product 1:N TransferItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product being transferred.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transfer item record was last updated.

StockMovement.title: StockMovement
StockMovement.description: Logs every change in product stock for a specific warehouse.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StockMovement entity.

productId.description: Reference to the Product involved in the movement. (Relationship: Product 1:N StockMovement)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse where the stock movement occurred. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N StockMovement)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product moved. Positive for incoming, negative for outgoing movements.

unitPrice.description: The unit price of the product at the time of movement.

referenceId.description: Reference to the originating transaction (GoodsReceipt, GoodsIssue, or Transfer).

referenceType.description: The type of the originating transaction (e.g., 'GoodsReceipt', 'GoodsIssue', 'Transfer').

currentStockLevel.description: The stock level of the product in the warehouse immediately after this movement.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the stock movement occurred.

responsibleUserId.description: Reference to the User who was responsible for the action causing this movement. (Relationship: User 1:N StockMovement)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the stock movement record was created.

Stock.title: Stock
Stock.description: Represents the current stock balance of a product in a specific warehouse, typically a materialized view or real-time aggregated data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Stock entity (combination of productId and warehouseId can form a natural key).

productId.description: Reference to the Product. (Relationship: Product 1:N Stock)

warehouseId.description: Reference to the Warehouse. (Relationship: Warehouse 1:N Stock)

currentQuantity.description: The current quantity of the product in this warehouse.

lastMovementTimestamp.description: Timestamp of the most recent stock movement that affected this balance.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this stock record was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this stock record was last updated.

AuditLog.title: AuditLog
AuditLog.description: Records all significant user activities and system changes for auditing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AuditLog entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the action. (Relationship: User 1:N AuditLog)

entityType.description: The type of entity that was affected by the action (e.g., 'Warehouse', 'Product', 'GoodsReceipt').

entityId.description: The ID of the entity that was affected.

oldValue.description: A description or JSON string representing the state of the entity before the action.

newValue.description: A description or JSON string representing the state of the entity after the action.

ipAddress.description: The IP address from which the action was initiated.

userAgent.description: The user agent string of the client that initiated the action.

definition.description: Defines application roles such as Admin, Warehouse Manager, and Operator. Access to manage roles is restricted to Admins. Users' assigned roles are queried from their User document for authorization checks.

Stores user profiles, including their assigned `roleId` and `warehouseId`. This crucial denormalization enables efficient role-based access control and warehouse-specific authorization checks directly from `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier for the user (typically Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Top-level collection for managing physical or logical warehouses. Documents include `responsiblePersonId` for initial ownership, and access for other roles (e.g., Warehouse Managers) is linked via their `warehouseId` in the `/users/{uid}` document.

Represents the current stock balance of a `Product` within a specific `Warehouse`. The `warehouseId` is implicit from the path, and `productId` serves as the document ID. This structure ensures clear, path-based access control for warehouse-specific stock. Includes `currentQuantity` and `lastMovementTimestamp`.

params.name: warehouseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent warehouse.

params.name: productId
params.description: Unique identifier for the product whose stock is being recorded.

definition.description: Global collection for organizing products into logical categories. Access to manage categories is typically controlled by user roles.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Global collection for managing vendor or supplier information. Access controlled by user roles.
Global collection for defining individual products managed in the inventory. Access controlled by user roles (e.g., Admins, Warehouse Managers can manage; Operators can read).
Records the process of receiving goods into a warehouse (Stock In). Includes denormalized `warehouseId` and `responsibleUserId` for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: goodsReceiptId
params.description: Unique identifier for the goods receipt transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products and quantities received within a GoodsReceipt. Includes denormalized `warehouseId` and `responsibleUserId` from the parent `GoodsReceipt` for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: goodsReceiptId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent goods receipt.

params.name: goodsReceiptItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the goods receipt item.

definition.description: Records the process of issuing goods from a warehouse (Stock Out). Includes denormalized `warehouseId` and `responsibleUserId` for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: goodsIssueId
params.description: Unique identifier for the goods issue transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products and quantities issued within a GoodsIssue. Includes denormalized `warehouseId` and `responsibleUserId` from the parent `GoodsIssue` for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: goodsIssueId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent goods issue.

params.name: goodsIssueItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the goods issue item.

definition.description: Records the movement of goods between two warehouses. Includes denormalized `sourceWarehouseId`, `destinationWarehouseId`, and `responsibleUserId` for authorization independence and efficient querying.

params.name: transferId
params.description: Unique identifier for the transfer transaction.

definition.description: Details individual products and quantities involved in a Transfer. Includes denormalized `sourceWarehouseId`, `destinationWarehouseId`, and `responsibleUserId` from the parent `Transfer` for authorization independence, allowing direct rule evaluation without parent lookups.

params.name: transferId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent transfer.

params.name: transferItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for the transfer item.

definition.description: Logs every change in product stock for auditing and reporting. Includes denormalized `warehouseId` and `responsibleUserId` for authorization independence. Primarily read-only for most users, with writes handled by backend functions.

params.name: stockMovementId
params.description: Unique identifier for the stock movement log entry.

definition.description: Records all significant user activities and system changes. Includes `userId`, `entityType`, and `entityId` for direct access control. Highly restricted (read-only for Admins), with writes exclusively via backend functions.

params.name: auditLogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore data structure for OmniStock prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query as a Privilege (QAP) by employing strategic denormalization and structural segregation. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Roles and Warehouse Assignments:** The `users` collection stores each user's `roleId` and their primary `warehouseId` directly in their document (`/users/{userId}`). This is critical for security rules, allowing a single `get(/users/$(request.auth.uid))` operation to retrieve a user's fundamental permissions without complex hierarchical lookups. This single `get()` is performed once per request, ensuring efficiency.
2.  **Denormalization in Subcollections:** For subcollections such as `goodsReceipts/{goodsReceiptId}/items`, `goodsIssues/{goodsIssueId}/items`, and `transfers/{transferId}/items`, key authorization context (e.g., `warehouseId`, `responsibleUserId`, `sourceWarehouseId`, `destinationWarehouseId`) from the parent document is **denormalized** into each individual item document. This eliminates the need for expensive and rule-breaking `get()` calls to parent documents when evaluating access for subcollection items. Security rules can directly check the denormalized fields on the `resource.data` of the item document, ensuring atomic authorization checks and robust security.
3.  **Top-level Transactions:** All top-level transactional documents (`goodsReceipts`, `goodsIssues`, `transfers`, `stockMovements`) explicitly contain the `warehouseId`(s) and `responsibleUserId` fields. This means any security rule evaluating access to these documents can directly use their own data (`resource.data`) for authorization, preventing chained `get()` operations.

**Query as a Privilege (QAPs):**
1.  **Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security:** Collections like `roles`, `categories`, `suppliers`, and `products` are at the root level, implying a global security posture where access is primarily role-based (e.g., only Admins can create/update, others can read). This simplifies security rules as all documents within these collections share the same access requirements, avoiding complex document-level filters that Firestore rules cannot enforce for `list` operations.
2.  **Path-Based Ownership for Warehouse Data:** The `warehouses/{warehouseId}/stock/{productId}` structure ensures that stock balances are directly nested under their respective warehouses. This naturally supports QAPs, as a Warehouse Manager assigned to `warehouseA` can only query (`list`) documents under `warehouses/warehouseA/stock`. The `warehouseId` is intrinsically part of the path, making rule evaluation straightforward (`allow read: if request.auth.uid == get(/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.warehouseId;`).
3.  **Denormalized Fields for Transactional QAPs:** For collections like `goodsReceipts`, `goodsIssues`, `transfers`, and `stockMovements`, the `warehouseId` (or source/destination warehouse IDs) is a field within each document. This enables secure `list` queries where a Warehouse Manager can filter for records pertaining to their assigned warehouse (e.g., `collection('goodsReceipts').where('warehouseId', '==', userWarehouseId)`). The security rules then confirm that `userWarehouseId` matches the `warehouseId` in the `resource.data` of the documents being queried, ensuring that only authorized data is returned, without the rules themselves acting as filters.
